goal: Let High priority auto-fixes proceed when blocked only by the old full-file line cap or prompt-based security fail-closed.
assumptions: Prompt-based SecurityCheck is useful as warning evidence but too noisy as a hard blocker.
Files over 300 lines are normal in the current frontend codebase.
Protected-file and allow-list fallback gates still provide necessary bounds.
risks: Full-file fallback becomes more powerful without a line cap.
Warn-only security findings must remain visible in outputs and comments.
steps: Add red e2e coverage for warn-only security push behavior.
Add red e2e coverage for large-file full-file fallback.
Remove SecurityCheck push blocking while preserving findings.
Remove the global full-file fallback line-count cap.
Run focused and full codex-cli verification.
